content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a linguistic 'reflexion' in the literal optical sense: just as a physical reflexion of light bounces off a surface to carry a flipped, modified version of the original input back to the observer, an LLM's self-reflection bounces its own output back onto its context window to surface inconsistent, perspective-shifted versions of its original reasoning. This literal-etymological bridge reveals that the multi-perspective inconsistency that makes modern LLM self-reflection methods like Self-Contrast effective is actually just the intentional flipping and reflection inherent to the core definition of 'reflexion' itself.
